title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs paths, file admission, root boundaries, and size budgets from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded file-admission policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Pathlike Contract · changed case · Review Pathlike Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Bookbot team handles case 2434 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review File Identity, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, File Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review File Identity, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 4767 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review File Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A filename uniquely identifies unchanged content over time.
